title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy and yoga for drug-refractory epilepsy: a randomized controlled trial
headline: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy and yoga for drug-refractory epilepsy: a randomized controlled trial
summary: A randomized controlled trial of yoga examined brain & mechanisms. The results indicate that both ACT and yoga significantly reduce seizure index and increase quality of life over time. The results of this study suggest that complementary treatments, such as ACT and yoga, decrease seizure index and increase quality of life.
keyFindings: The results indicate that both ACT and yoga significantly reduce seizure index and increase quality of life over time.
The results of this study suggest that complementary treatments, such as ACT and yoga, decrease seizure index and increase quality of life.
limitations: Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (yoga) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy and yoga for drug-refractory epilepsy: a randomized controlled trial
aiSnippet: RCT, Epilepsy & behavior : E&B (2008). The results indicate that both ACT and yoga significantly reduce seizure index and increase quality of life over time. The results of this study suggest that complementary treatments, such as ACT and yoga, decrease seizure index and increase quality of life.
mechanismGap: The practice in this study does not share a specific breath-regulation or directed-attention mechanism with the Miracle of Mind practice, so no practice bridge is drawn here.
